prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `642d658d`.
codex_response:
Implemented `642d658d` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: find every plus centered on `4` whose four direct neighbors are all the same non-`4` color, then output the modal petal color as a `1x1` grid. The `opus46` summary was consistent with the official examples, so I used it after verification; no `notes.md` was needed.

The task package is in [arc2/tasks/task_642d658d/__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_642d658d/__init__.py), [arc2/tasks/task_642d658d/verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_642d658d/verifier.py), and [arc2/tasks/task_642d658d/generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_642d658d/generator.py). The generator matches the family by constructing large dominant-color boards with structured `0` holes, several center-`4` flowers where exactly one petal color repeats twice, and many singleton distractors whose raw frequencies can disagree with the answer. No new primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end:
- `python -m arc2.shared.verify --task 642d658d` -> `4/4` official examples passed
- `python -m arc2.shared.build --task 642d658d --n-examples 1000` -> built artifacts
- `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 642d658d` -> `1000/1000` generated examples passed

Artifacts are at [arc2/artifacts/642d658d/task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/642d658d/task.json), [arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/originals.png), [arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_01.png), [arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_02.png), and [arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_03.png). I also inspected [arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/642d658d/previews/preview_01.png); it looks on-family.
